commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a direct ACP runtime fallback after unhandled `reply_dispatch` for ACP session keys and adds a regression test for configured Telegram-style ACP bindings.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +68. Total +115 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a focused source/unit reproduction: current main relies on `reply_dispatch` claiming ACP sessions, and the PR adds a test for a configured Telegram-style ACP session when that hook declines. I did not establish a live Telegram/acpx reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(reply): keep configured ACP bindings from dropping the first turn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #97186.

Configured `type: "acp"` bindings could finish session initialization but still lose the first inbound turn when no `reply_dispatch` hook claimed the ACP session. The result matched the report: session created, backend process spawned, but no `systemSent`, no transcript, and no visible reply.

## What Problem This Solves

Telegram-style ACP sessions can be routed to a configured ACP target session before any reply-dispatch hook claims the event. When that hook path declines the event, the first user turn is silently dropped instead of being dispatched to the ACP runtime, so the conversation never produces a visible reply.

## What changed

- Build the ACP `reply_dispatch` event once and reuse it.
- If the hook path does not handle an ACP-bound session, fall back to calling the core ACP dispatch runtime directly before embedded-model fallback.
- Add a regression test that uses a configured Telegram-style ACP session key and proves the first turn still reaches `runTurn()` even when the hook declines to claim it.

## Why this fixes the bug

Configured ACP bindings already rewrite the inbound session key to the ACP target session. The missing piece was a core fallback for that target when hook wiring was absent or declined the event. With this change, ACP-bound sessions no longer depend on the hook path to process the first prompt.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
- `pnpm_config_verify_deps_before_run=false pnpm build`
